words.def: something that you say or write which expresses an opinion, a thought, etc. about somebody/something
words.example: to make a remark

words.def: unusual or surprising in a way that causes people to take notice
words.example: a remarkable achievement/career/talent

words.def: in a way that is unusual or surprising and causes people to take notice
words.example: The car is in remarkably good condition for its age.

words.def: the presenting of and writing about news on television and radio, and in newspapers
words.example: accurate/balanced/objective reporting

words.def: typical of a particular group of people; that is a typical example of something
words.example: Is a questionnaire answered by 500 people truly representative of the population as a whole?

words.def: the opinion that people have about what somebody/something is like, based on what has happened in the past
words.example: to have a good/bad reputation

words.def: something that you need or want
words.example: the basic requirements of life

words.def: the act of saving somebody/something from a dangerous or difficult situation; the fact of being saved
words.example: We had given up hope of rescue.

words.def: a supply of something that is available to be used in the future or when it is needed
words.example: cash/foreign currency reserves

words.def: living in a particular place
words.example: the town’s resident population (= not tourists or visitors)

words.def: to officially tell somebody that you are leaving your job, an organization, etc.
words.example: She was forced to resign due to ill health.

words.def: to refuse to accept something and try to stop it from happening
words.example: resist something, They are determined to resist pressure to change the law.

words.def: a definite decision to do or not to do something
words.example: Have you made any New Year's resolutions (= for example, to give up smoking from 1 January)?

words.def: to find an acceptable solution to a problem or difficulty
words.example: resolve something, to resolve an issue/a dispute/a conflict/a crisis

words.def: a place where a lot of people go on holiday
words.example: seaside/mountain resorts

words.def: to bring back a situation or feeling that existed before
words.example: restore something, The measures are intended to restore public confidence in the economy.

words.def: to limit the size, amount or range of something
words.example: restrict something to something, Speed is restricted to 30 mph in towns.

words.def: a rule or law that limits what you can do or what can happen
words.example: import/speed/travel restrictions

words.def: the selling of goods to the public, usually through shops
words.example: The recommended retail price is £9.99.

words.def: to keep something; to continue to have something
words.example: to retain your independence

words.def: the fact of leaving your job and stopping work, usually because you have reached a particular age; the time when you do this
words.example: At 60, he was now approaching retirement.

words.def: to make something known to somebody
words.example: reveal something, to reveal a secret

words.def: the money that a government receives from taxes or that an organization, etc. receives from its business
words.example: The government is currently facing a shortfall in tax revenue.

words.def: a change or set of changes to something
words.example: He made some minor revisions to the report before printing it out.

words.def: an attempt, by a large number of people, to change the government of a country, especially by violent action
words.example: a socialist revolution

words.def: a thing that you are given because you have done something good, worked hard, etc.
words.example: a financial reward

words.def: a strong regular repeated pattern of sounds or movements
words.example: to dance to the rhythm of the music

words.def: to be free of somebody/something that has been annoying you or that you do not want
words.example: She wanted to be rid of her parents and their authority.

words.def: very silly or unreasonable
words.example: I look ridiculous in this hat.

words.def: involving the possibility of something bad happening
words.example: Life as an aid worker can be a risky business (= dangerous).

words.def: competing with another person, company, thing, etc.
words.example: a rival bid/claim/offer

words.def: to steal money or property from a person or place
words.example: to rob a bank

words.def: the crime of stealing money or goods from a bank, shop, person, etc., especially using violence or threats
words.example: armed robbery (= using a gun, knife, etc.)

words.def: a spacecraft in the shape of a tube that is driven by a stream of gases let out behind it when fuel is burned inside
words.example: a space rocket

words.def: an exciting, usually short, relationship between two people who are in love with each other
words.example: a holiday romance

words.def: the part of a plant that grows under the ground and takes in water and minerals that it sends to the rest of the plant
words.example: deep spreading roots

words.def: a flower with a sweet smell that grows on a bush with thorns (= sharp points) on its stems
words.example: a bunch of red roses

words.def: approximately but not exactly
words.example: Sales are up by roughly 10 per cent.

words.def: having a shape like a circle or a ball
words.example: a round plate

words.def: a way that you follow to get from one place to another
words.example: Which is the best route to take?

words.def: done or happening as a normal part of a particular job, situation or process
words.example: routine enquiries/questions/tests/screening

words.def: to move your hand, a cloth, etc., backwards and forwards over a surface while pressing it
words.example: rub something, He sat up on the hard bunk and rubbed his eyes.

words.def: made of rubber
words.example: a rubber ball

words.def: the state or process of being destroyed or severely damaged
words.example: A large number of churches fell into ruin after the revolution.

words.def: connected with or like the countryside
words.example: rural areas

words.def: a sudden strong movement or action
words.example: rush for something, Shoppers made a rush for the exits.

words.def: a sheet of strong cloth which the wind blows against to make a boat or ship travel through the water
words.example: As the boat moved down the river the wind began to fill the sails.

words.def: money that employees receive for doing their job, especially professional employees or people working in an office, usually paid every month
words.example: an annual salary of $40 000

words.def: a number of people or things taken from a larger group and used in tests to provide information about the group
words.example: The interviews were given to a random sample of students.

words.def: an electronic device that is sent into space and moves around the earth or another planet. It is used for communicating by radio, television, etc. and for gathering information.
words.example: a weather/communications/spy satellite

words.def: the good feeling that you have when you have achieved something or when something that you wanted to happen does happen; something that gives you this feeling
words.example: to gain/get/derive satisfaction from something

words.def: pleased because you have achieved something or because something that you wanted to happen has happened
words.example: a satisfied customer

words.def: to make somebody pleased by doing or giving them what they want
words.example: Nothing satisfies him—he's always complaining.

words.def: an amount of something such as time or money that you do not need to use or spend
words.example: saving of something, Buy three and make a saving of 55p.

words.def: the size or extent of something, especially when compared with something else
words.example: on a … scale, They entertain on a large scale (= they hold expensive parties with a lot of guests).

words.def: behaviour or an event that people think is morally or legally wrong and causes public feelings of shock or anger
words.example: a series of sex scandals

words.def: a situation in which a lot of people are anxious or frightened about something
words.example: a health scare

words.def: a description of how things might happen in the future
words.example: Let me suggest a possible scenario.

words.def: a plan that lists all the work that you have to do and when you must do each thing
words.example: I have a hectic schedule for the next few days.
